ext.description: A warm, paper-like VS Code theme inspired by Claude's design language — with a dynamic preset system and per-language color control.
config.title: Terracotta
config.colorPreset.description: Global syntax highlighting color preset. Changes apply instantly without reloading.
config.colorPreset.warm: Warm neutral tones, clay orange (#D97757) as the primary accent.
config.colorPreset.claude: High-fidelity replica of Claude.ai's web interface syntax highlighting.
config.colorPreset.custom: Use your own colors defined in terracotta.customColors.
config.languages.description:
Per-language preset overrides. Keys are VS Code language IDs (e.g. javascript, typescript, python, rust, go, css, html). Values are 'warm', 'claude', 'custom', or 'follow-global' to inherit the global preset.

Example: { "python": "claude", "css": "custom" }
config.customColors.description: Individual token color overrides used when preset is 'custom' (globally or per-language). Unset categories fall back to the 'warm' preset. Values must be hex color strings (e.g. '#D97757').
config.customColors.keywords: Keywords and storage modifiers — const, function, import, class, etc.
config.customColors.controlFlow: Control flow keywords — if, else, for, while, return, etc.
config.customColors.types: Type names, classes, interfaces, structs, enums.
config.customColors.properties: Object properties and record field names.
config.customColors.tags: HTML / JSX / CSS tag and selector names.
config.italicKeywords.description: Render keywords and storage modifiers in italic style. Best paired with a font that has distinct italic characters (e.g. Operator Mono, Fira Code iScript).
config.boldHeadings.description: Render Markdown and LaTeX headings in bold.
config.enableDynamicColors.description: When disabled, the extension stops auto-updating editor.tokenColorCustomizations. Useful if you want to manually fine-tune the injected rules without them being overwritten.
command.switchPreset.title: Switch Color Preset
walkthrough.title: Get started with Terracotta
walkthrough.description: Set up your perfect warm editor in a few steps.
walkthrough.step1.title: Choose your color preset
walkthrough.step1.description: Terracotta comes with two built-in palettes and a fully custom option. [Open settings](command:workbench.action.openSettings?%22terracotta.colorPreset%22) or run **Terracotta: Switch Color Preset** from the Command Palette.
walkthrough.step2.title: Override per language
walkthrough.step2.description: Use a different palette just for specific languages. [Configure languages](command:workbench.action.openSettings?%22terracotta.languages%22)
walkthrough.step3.title: Fine-tune with custom colors
walkthrough.step3.description: Set individual token colors when the 'custom' preset is active. [Open custom colors](command:workbench.action.openSettings?%22terracotta.customColors%22)
